items.title: CMS issues interim final rule requiring Medicaid work requirements for many adults
items.summary: CMS released an interim final rule that requires certain Medicaid applicants and enrollees ages 19–64 to meet a community engagement requirement of 80 hours per month through work, education, work programs, or community service. CMS says the requirement must be implemented no later than January 1, 2027, and lists exemptions including pregnancy/postpartum status, disability or medical frailty, American Indian/Alaska Native status, and certain caregivers.

items.title: USDA opens FY 2026 SNAP technology grants; applications due June 29
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service says up to $5 million is available for FY 2026 SNAP Process and Technology Improvement Grants, with an anticipated 6 to 12 awards. Applications are due to Grants.gov by 11:59 p.m. EDT on June 29, 2026, and prior PTIG lead recipients from FY 2024 or FY 2025 are not eligible to apply as the lead entity.

items.title: USDA seeks public comments on SNAP data collections; deadline June 22
items.summary: USDA Food and Nutrition Service is taking public comments on a SNAP information collection that includes items on major changes in program design and interstate data matching to prevent multiple issuances. The agency says comments received by June 22, 2026 will be considered.

items.title: USDA final rule expands whole and reduced-fat milk options in child nutrition programs
items.summary: USDA issued a final rule effective June 8, 2026 allowing child nutrition program operators to offer whole and reduced-fat milk to participants age 2 and older in programs including NSLP, SBP, CACFP, and the Special Milk Program. USDA says comments on the final rule must be received on or before June 8, 2026, and the rule also clarifies updated milk options by age group.

items.title: HUD sets Tribal HUD-VASH renewal requirements; renewal packages due June 5
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-14 sets renewal requirements for Tribal HUD-VASH recipients, with renewal packages due electronically by 11:59:59 p.m. ET on June 5, 2026. The notice says renewal funding includes $10 million provided in the FY 2026 Consolidated Appropriations Act, and the renewal period of performance is October 1, 2026 through September 30, 2027.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: IRS: Free File remains available through Oct. 15 for 2025 tax returns
items.summary: The IRS reminded taxpayers that they can still file a 2025 federal return at no cost using IRS Free File. The IRS says taxpayers with adjusted gross income of $89,000 or less can use guided Free File software, and IRS Free File remains available through Oct. 15.

items.title: IRS: ‘Trump Accounts’ cannot be funded before July 4, 2026
items.summary: In its summary of the One, Big, Beautiful Bill Act, the IRS says ‘Trump Accounts’ cannot be funded before July 4, 2026. The IRS also says the federal government will make a one-time $1,000 contribution for each eligible child’s account and that annual contributions are authorized up to $5,000 per year.
